subject: Geography
title: The 38th Parallel
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The 38th Parallel.

hook.text: In 1945, two US officers split Korea along the 38th parallel using a National Geographic map.

buildup.text: They had 30 minutes to divide the peninsula between US and Soviet occupation zones.

discovery.text: The line was meant to be temporary. It became the border between two hostile nations.

twist.text: The Korean War pushed the line slightly but ended almost exactly where it started.

climax.text: Today, the DMZ along this line is one of the most heavily militarized borders on Earth.

punchline.text: A 30-minute decision split a nation for 80 years and counting.

quiz.question: How was the Korean border originally chosen?
options: Two officers picked a line on a map in 30 minutes
A UN committee spent years negotiating
It followed a natural mountain range
